title: Lists & Dictionaries
subtitle: Create, Access, and Modify Collections
overview: Students will learn how to store multiple values using lists and dictionaries. They will practice creating, accessing, updating, adding, and removing data from collections using Python.

images._note: Add screenshots of Python code, collection examples, and student practice activities.

activity.title: Create a Student Record System
steps: Create a list of favorite fruits
Print the first item in the list
Add a new fruit using append()
Remove an item from the list
Create a dictionary for student information
Add a new key called city

quiz.q: What is a list in Python?
options: A variable type only for text
quiz.explanation: Lists store multiple items in a specific order.

quiz.q: Which symbol is used to create a list?
quiz.explanation: Lists are created using square brackets [].

quiz.q: What is the index of the first item in a list?
Which method adds an item to a list?
quiz.explanation: append() adds a new item to the end of a list.

quiz.q: Which method removes an item from a list?
quiz.explanation: remove() deletes a specified item from a list.

quiz.q: What is a dictionary in Python?
quiz.explanation: Dictionaries store information as key-value pairs.

quiz.q: Which symbol is used to create a dictionary?
quiz.explanation: Dictionaries are created using curly braces {}.

quiz.q: How do you access a value in a dictionary?
quiz.explanation: Dictionary values are accessed through keys.
Index 0 refers to the first item in a list.

quiz.q: Which collection keeps items in key-value format?
quiz.explanation: Dictionaries organize data using keys and values.

quiz.q: How can you change an item in a list?
quiz.explanation: List items can be updated using their index.
append() adds a new item at the end of the list.

quiz.q: Which collection is best for storing student name and marks?
quiz.explanation: A dictionary stores related information using keys and values.

quiz.q: What is the output of len([1,2,3,4])?
quiz.explanation: len() returns the total number of items in the list.

quiz.q: Why are lists and dictionaries useful?
options: They organize and manage data efficiently
quiz.explanation: Collections help programmers store and manage multiple pieces of data easily.
